items.title: Security update available for Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source
items.summary: This update resolves critical, important, and moderate vulnerabilities that could lead to arbitrary code execution, security feature bypass, and privilege escalation. Eight of the 14 vulnerabilities addressed are rated Critical, with the highest CVSS score of 9.6.

items.title: Critical vulnerability in Amasty extensions leading to Remote Code Execution
items.summary: Amasty issued a massive security release for 25 extensions, with at least one critical vulnerability that can lead to remote code execution. Sansec Shield already blocks the critical issues.

items.title: Unauthenticated arbitrary file upload in Amasty Order Attributes for Magento
items.summary: An unauthenticated arbitrary file upload flaw in Amasty Order Attributes allows attackers to write executable files to a Magento store without logging in, potentially leading to remote code execution.

items.title: Unauthenticated PHP object injection in Mirasvit Cache Warmer for Magento
items.summary: Sansec discovered a critical (CVSS 9.8) unauthenticated PHP object injection vulnerability in Mirasvit Cache Warmer, a popular Magento extension, which can lead to remote code execution. Mirasvit released a patched version (1.11.12) on May 25, 2026, and CISA has added this flaw to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog.

items.title: Security update available for Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source
items.summary: This update resolves critical, important, and moderate vulnerabilities that could lead to arbitrary code execution, arbitrary file system write, application denial-of-service, and security feature bypass. Adobe is not aware of any exploits in the wild for these issues.

items.title: Unrestricted file upload in Magento and Adobe Commerce REST API
items.summary: Sansec discovered PolyShell, a critical flaw in Magento's REST API that allows unauthenticated attackers to upload executable files to any store by disguising code as an image. While Adobe released Magento 2.4.9 with a fix, older supported versions remain vulnerable.

items.title: Security update available for Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source
items.summary: This security update addresses critical, important, and moderate vulnerabilities that could lead to security feature bypass, application denial-of-service, privilege escalation, arbitrary code execution, and arbitrary file system read. Adobe recommends applying security patches as quickly as possible.
